samples.image_requirement: Gate, loading dock, factory road, or restricted lane with clear vehicle path and access boundary.
samples.prompt: FORGE-Bench evaluation prompt. Domain x Task cell: domain=visual_security; abstract_task=industrial_logic_and_compliance. Task objective: test industrial_logic_and_compliance capability inside the visual_security industrial domain. Scene family: vsec_unregistered_vehicle_intrusion. Core scenario: Unregistered third-party truck enters a marked restricted industrial zone; show detection, alarm, and security response. Reference subject: industrial gate vehicle restricted zone. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: hold a fixed monitoring view; no camera movement; target motion value 0.0. Evaluation dimensions: industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment, geometric_integrity, physical_plausibility, temporal_consistency, reference_and_motion_fidelity. Industrial logic and fact alignment: use state-machine style reasoning to verify causal closure, conditional triggers such as alarms or braking, compliance state, equipment roles, personnel/vehicle states, and industrial fact progression. Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: Gate, loading dock, factory road, or restricted lane with clear vehicle path and access boundary. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.05, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.85, physical_plausibility=1.25, reference_and_motion_fidelity=0.95, temporal_consistency=1.35.
samples.video_generation_prompt: Use the provided reference image as the first frame and visual anchor. Generate a 5-8 second realistic industrial video. FORGE-Bench Domain x Task cell: visual_security x industrial_logic_and_compliance. Scene: Unregistered third-party truck enters a marked restricted industrial zone; show detection, alarm, and security response. Reference subject: industrial gate vehicle restricted zone. Camera and motion: hold a fixed monitoring view; no camera movement; target motion value 0.0. Action: Show the violation or trigger followed by the correct industrial response such as alarm, braking, stop, evacuation, lockout, or escalation. Optimize for the five evaluation dimensions: industrial logic, geometry, physics, temporal consistency, and reference/motion fidelity. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

axis_rubric.physical_plausibility: Emergency braking, intrusion, contact, explosion, or fall response must be physically plausible.

samples.application_value: Safety monitoring, access control, PPE compliance, emergency stop, alarm triggering, and industrial rule-following videos.

axis_rubric.industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment: Highest priority: violation, trigger, alarm, stop, evacuation, or consequence must form a complete causal loop.
axis_rubric.temporal_consistency: Personnel, vehicles, zones, alarms, and stopped equipment must keep identity across the event.
axis_rubric.physical_plausibility: Emergency braking, intrusion, contact, explosion, or fall response must be physically plausible.

Does the generated event remain consistent with domain visual_security and task category industrial_logic_and_compliance?

samples.prompt: FORGE-Bench evaluation prompt. Domain x Task cell: domain=visual_security; abstract_task=industrial_logic_and_compliance. Task objective: test industrial_logic_and_compliance capability inside the visual_security industrial domain. Scene family: vsec_missing_ppe_at_height. Core scenario: Worker performs high-altitude work without helmet or harness; show violation recognition and stoppage/escalation. Reference subject: worker aerial work platform safety harness. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: hold a fixed monitoring view; no camera movement; target motion value 0.0. Evaluation dimensions: industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment, geometric_integrity, physical_plausibility, temporal_consistency, reference_and_motion_fidelity. Industrial logic and fact alignment: use state-machine style reasoning to verify causal closure, conditional triggers such as alarms or braking, compliance state, equipment roles, personnel/vehicle states, and industrial fact progression. Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: Elevated work platform, scaffold, roof edge, or harness zone with visible worker safety context. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.05, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.85, physical_plausibility=1.25, reference_and_motion_fidelity=0.95, temporal_consistency=1.35.
samples.video_generation_prompt: Use the provided reference image as the first frame and visual anchor. Generate a 5-8 second realistic industrial video. FORGE-Bench Domain x Task cell: visual_security x industrial_logic_and_compliance. Scene: Worker performs high-altitude work without helmet or harness; show violation recognition and stoppage/escalation. Reference subject: worker aerial work platform safety harness. Camera and motion: hold a fixed monitoring view; no camera movement; target motion value 0.0. Action: Show the violation or trigger followed by the correct industrial response such as alarm, braking, stop, evacuation, lockout, or escalation. Optimize for the five evaluation dimensions: industrial logic, geometry, physics, temporal consistency, and reference/motion fidelity. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

samples.prompt: FORGE-Bench evaluation prompt. Domain x Task cell: domain=visual_security; abstract_task=industrial_logic_and_compliance. Task objective: test industrial_logic_and_compliance capability inside the visual_security industrial domain. Scene family: vsec_missing_ppe_at_height. Core scenario: Worker performs high-altitude work without helmet or harness; show violation recognition and stoppage/escalation. Reference subject: worker aerial work platform safety harness. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: hold a fixed monitoring view; no camera movement; target motion value 0.0. Evaluation dimensions: industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment, geometric_integrity, physical_plausibility, temporal_consistency, reference_and_motion_fidelity. Industrial logic and fact alignment: use state-machine style reasoning to verify causal closure, conditional triggers such as alarms or braking, compliance state, equipment roles, personnel/vehicle states, and industrial fact progression. Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: Elevated work platform, scaffold, roof edge, or harness zone with visible worker safety context. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.05, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.85, physical_plausibility=1.25, reference_and_motion_fidelity=0.95, temporal_consistency=1.35.

Task objective: test rigid_body_kinematics_and_coupling capability inside the visual_security industrial domain. Scene family: vsec_crane_unsafe_swing_near_people. Core scenario: Crane slews a suspended load too close to personnel; preserve cable/load path and show unsafe swing consequence. Reference subject: crane suspended load exclusion zone. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: perform a slow horizontal pan while the mechanism operates; target motion value horizontal_pan_lr. Evaluation dimensions: industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment, geometric_integrity, physical_plausibility, temporal_consistency, reference_and_motion_fidelity. Industrial logic and fact alignment: use state-machine style reasoning to verify causal closure, conditional triggers such as alarms or braking, compliance state, equipment roles, personnel/vehicle states, and industrial fact progression. Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: Mobile crane, tower crane, or suspended hook/load with visible exclusion zone. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.70, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.10, physical_plausibility=1.45, reference_and_motion_fidelity=0.85, temporal_consistency=1.25.
samples.video_generation_prompt: Use the provided reference image as the first frame and visual anchor. Generate a 5-8 second realistic industrial video. FORGE-Bench Domain x Task cell: visual_security x rigid_body_kinematics_and_coupling. Scene: Crane slews a suspended load too close to personnel; preserve cable/load path and show unsafe swing consequence. Reference subject: crane suspended load exclusion zone. Camera and motion: perform a slow horizontal pan while the mechanism operates; target motion value horizontal_pan_lr. Action: Show the requested rigid mechanism or load interaction with all links, joints, supports, contact points, and load paths staying physically coupled. Optimize for the five evaluation dimensions: industrial logic, geometry, physics, temporal consistency, and reference/motion fidelity. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

axis_rubric.temporal_consistency: The same mechanism and parts must persist without flicker, detachment, identity swap, or soft-body melting.

samples.prompt: FORGE-Bench evaluation prompt. Domain x Task cell: domain=visual_security; abstract_task=topology_mutation_and_failure. Task objective: test topology_mutation_and_failure capability inside the visual_security industrial domain. Scene family: vsec_perimeter_fence_breach. Core scenario: Restricted-zone fence is broken by impact; only the damaged section opens while the rest of the barrier stays fixed. Reference subject: industrial perimeter fence gate. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: perform a controlled dolly-in toward the local defect or failure region; target motion value 1.5. Evaluation dimensions: industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment, geometric_integrity, physical_plausibility, temporal_consistency, reference_and_motion_fidelity. Industrial logic and fact alignment: use state-machine style reasoning to verify causal closure, conditional triggers such as alarms or braking, compliance state, equipment roles, personnel/vehicle states, and industrial fact progression. Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: Industrial fence, gate, barrier, or guardrail with localized area for damage. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.80, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.10, physical_plausibility=1.05, reference_and_motion_fidelity=1.35, temporal_consistency=1.25.

Core scenario: Restricted-zone fence is broken by impact; only the damaged section opens while the rest of the barrier stays fixed. Reference subject: industrial perimeter fence gate. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: perform a controlled dolly-in toward the local defect or failure region; target motion value 1.5. Evaluation dimensions: industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment, geometric_integrity, physical_plausibility, temporal_consistency, reference_and_motion_fidelity. Industrial logic and fact alignment: use state-machine style reasoning to verify causal closure, conditional triggers such as alarms or braking, compliance state, equipment roles, personnel/vehicle states, and industrial fact progression. Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: Industrial fence, gate, barrier, or guardrail with localized area for damage. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.80, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.10, physical_plausibility=1.05, reference_and_motion_fidelity=1.35, temporal_consistency=1.25.
samples.video_generation_prompt: Use the provided reference image as the first frame and visual anchor. Generate a 5-8 second realistic industrial video. FORGE-Bench Domain x Task cell: visual_security x topology_mutation_and_failure. Scene: Restricted-zone fence is broken by impact; only the damaged section opens while the rest of the barrier stays fixed. Reference subject: industrial perimeter fence gate. Camera and motion: perform a controlled dolly-in toward the local defect or failure region; target motion value 1.5. Action: Generate only the requested local break, defect, deformation, missing element, or structural failure while untouched regions remain locked. Optimize for the five evaluation dimensions: industrial logic, geometry, physics, temporal consistency, and reference/motion fidelity. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

samples.prompt: FORGE-Bench evaluation prompt. Domain x Task cell: domain=visual_security; abstract_task=industrial_logic_and_compliance. Task objective: test industrial_logic_and_compliance capability inside the visual_security industrial domain. Scene family: vsec_pedestrian_forklift_near_miss. Core scenario: Pedestrian enters forklift lane; show warning, braking, and near-miss avoidance without identity swaps. Reference subject: warehouse forklift pedestrian lane. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: hold a fixed monitoring view; no camera movement; target motion value 0.0. Evaluation dimensions: industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment, geometric_integrity, physical_plausibility, temporal_consistency, reference_and_motion_fidelity. Industrial logic and fact alignment: use state-machine style reasoning to verify causal closure, conditional triggers such as alarms or braking, compliance state, equipment roles, personnel/vehicle states, and industrial fact progression. Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: Warehouse aisle or yard intersection with forklifts/pedestrian route and readable right-of-way context. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.05, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.85, physical_plausibility=1.25, reference_and_motion_fidelity=0.95, temporal_consistency=1.35.

Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: Industrial corridor, workshop, battery room, or equipment bay with alarm/exit context. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.05, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.25, physical_plausibility=1.85, reference_and_motion_fidelity=0.95, temporal_consistency=1.30.

samples.prompt: FORGE-Bench evaluation prompt. Domain x Task cell: domain=visual_security; abstract_task=topology_mutation_and_failure. Task objective: test topology_mutation_and_failure capability inside the visual_security industrial domain. Scene family: vsec_guard_removed_conveyor. Core scenario: A machine guard is missing or displaced near a conveyor; preserve machine topology and show the unsafe exposed zone. Reference subject: conveyor machine guard. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: perform a controlled dolly-in toward the local defect or failure region; target motion value 1.5. Evaluation dimensions: industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment, geometric_integrity, physical_plausibility, temporal_consistency, reference_and_motion_fidelity. Industrial logic and fact alignment: use state-machine style reasoning to verify causal closure, conditional triggers such as alarms or braking, compliance state, equipment roles, personnel/vehicle states, and industrial fact progression. Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: Conveyor, rotating machinery, guard cover, or exposed pinch-point area. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.80, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.10, physical_plausibility=1.05, reference_and_motion_fidelity=1.35, temporal_consistency=1.25.
samples.video_generation_prompt: Use the provided reference image as the first frame and visual anchor. Generate a 5-8 second realistic industrial video. FORGE-Bench Domain x Task cell: visual_security x topology_mutation_and_failure. Scene: A machine guard is missing or displaced near a conveyor; preserve machine topology and show the unsafe exposed zone. Reference subject: conveyor machine guard. Camera and motion: perform a controlled dolly-in toward the local defect or failure region; target motion value 1.5. Action: Generate only the requested local break, defect, deformation, missing element, or structural failure while untouched regions remain locked. Optimize for the five evaluation dimensions: industrial logic, geometry, physics, temporal consistency, and reference/motion fidelity. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

Evaluation dimensions: industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment, geometric_integrity, physical_plausibility, temporal_consistency, reference_and_motion_fidelity. Industrial logic and fact alignment: use state-machine style reasoning to verify causal closure, conditional triggers such as alarms or braking, compliance state, equipment roles, personnel/vehicle states, and industrial fact progression. Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: Industrial robot arm with gripper/tool and reachable workpiece. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.70, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.10, physical_plausibility=1.45, reference_and_motion_fidelity=0.85, temporal_consistency=1.25.

axis_rubric.physical_plausibility: Emergency braking, intrusion, contact, explosion, or fall response must be physically plausible.

industrial_logic_questions.text: Does the video preserve the cause-and-effect chain for this scenario: Cobot slows and yields during human handover; show safe speed change and no unsafe contact?
Does any core equipment, person, vehicle, defect region, or emergency state change role without a visible causal trigger?
Does the generated event remain consistent with domain embodied_robotics and task category industrial_logic_and_compliance?

sensitivity_variants.prompt_delta: Use slower motion and a wider view with fewer interacting elements.
Use tighter framing, stronger motion, and more occlusion while preserving all constraints.

samples.task_title_zh: 协作机器人与人员安全交接

samples.image_requirement: Collaborative robot workstation with human proximity or handover table.
samples.prompt: FORGE-Bench evaluation prompt. Domain x Task cell: domain=embodied_robotics; abstract_task=industrial_logic_and_compliance. Task objective: test industrial_logic_and_compliance capability inside the embodied_robotics industrial domain. Scene family: erob_cobot_human_handover. Core scenario: Cobot slows and yields during human handover; show safe speed change and no unsafe contact. Reference subject: collaborative robot workstation. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: hold a fixed monitoring view; no camera movement; target motion value 0.0. Evaluation dimensions: industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment, geometric_integrity, physical_plausibility, temporal_consistency, reference_and_motion_fidelity. Industrial logic and fact alignment: use state-machine style reasoning to verify causal closure, conditional triggers such as alarms or braking, compliance state, equipment roles, personnel/vehicle states, and industrial fact progression. Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: Collaborative robot workstation with human proximity or handover table. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.05, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.85, physical_plausibility=1.25, reference_and_motion_fidelity=0.95, temporal_consistency=1.35.
samples.video_generation_prompt: Use the provided reference image as the first frame and visual anchor. Generate a 5-8 second realistic industrial video. FORGE-Bench Domain x Task cell: embodied_robotics x industrial_logic_and_compliance. Scene: Cobot slows and yields during human handover; show safe speed change and no unsafe contact. Reference subject: collaborative robot workstation. Camera and motion: hold a fixed monitoring view; no camera movement; target motion value 0.0. Action: Show the violation or trigger followed by the correct industrial response such as alarm, braking, stop, evacuation, lockout, or escalation. Optimize for the five evaluation dimensions: industrial logic, geometry, physics, temporal consistency, and reference/motion fidelity. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

samples.prompt: FORGE-Bench evaluation prompt. Domain x Task cell: domain=embodied_robotics; abstract_task=spatial_exploration_and_viewpoint. Task objective: test spatial_exploration_and_viewpoint capability inside the embodied_robotics industrial domain. Scene family: erob_tracked_robot_rubble. Core scenario: Tracked robot climbs rubble while first-person camera maintains stable depth and obstacle geometry. Reference subject: tracked inspection robot rubble. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: perform a smooth left-to-right inspection pan; target motion value horizontal_pan_lr. Evaluation dimensions: industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment, geometric_integrity, physical_plausibility, temporal_consistency, reference_and_motion_fidelity. Industrial logic and fact alignment: use state-machine style reasoning to verify causal closure, conditional triggers such as alarms or braking, compliance state, equipment roles, personnel/vehicle states, and industrial fact progression. Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: Tracked robot, inspection crawler, or rescue robot near debris, pipes, or uneven terrain. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.35, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.00, physical_plausibility=1.00, reference_and_motion_fidelity=1.75, temporal_consistency=1.25.
samples.video_generation_prompt: Use the provided reference image as the first frame and visual anchor. Generate a 5-8 second realistic industrial video. FORGE-Bench Domain x Task cell: embodied_robotics x spatial_exploration_and_viewpoint. Scene: Tracked robot climbs rubble while first-person camera maintains stable depth and obstacle geometry. Reference subject: tracked inspection robot rubble. Camera and motion: perform a smooth left-to-right inspection pan; target motion value horizontal_pan_lr. Action: Execute the requested camera or embodied viewpoint motion while keeping equipment identity, spatial scale, and visible geometry stable. Optimize for the five evaluation dimensions: industrial logic, geometry, physics, temporal consistency, and reference/motion fidelity. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

samples.image_requirement: Quadruped robot or legged robot in industrial stairs, tunnel, plant, or debris setting.
samples.prompt: FORGE-Bench evaluation prompt. Domain x Task cell: domain=embodied_robotics; abstract_task=spatial_exploration_and_viewpoint. Task objective: test spatial_exploration_and_viewpoint capability inside the embodied_robotics industrial domain. Scene family: erob_quadruped_stairs_rubble_fpv. Core scenario: Quadruped head camera traverses complex rubble/stairs without body identity changes or impossible foot contacts. Reference subject: quadruped robot industrial stairs. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: perform a slow dolly-in revealing the inspection target; target motion value 1.5. Evaluation dimensions: industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment, geometric_integrity, physical_plausibility, temporal_consistency, reference_and_motion_fidelity. Industrial logic and fact alignment: use state-machine style reasoning to verify causal closure, conditional triggers such as alarms or braking, compliance state, equipment roles, personnel/vehicle states, and industrial fact progression. Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: Quadruped robot or legged robot in industrial stairs, tunnel, plant, or debris setting. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.35, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.00, physical_plausibility=1.00, reference_and_motion_fidelity=1.75, temporal_consistency=1.25.
samples.video_generation_prompt: Use the provided reference image as the first frame and visual anchor. Generate a 5-8 second realistic industrial video. FORGE-Bench Domain x Task cell: embodied_robotics x spatial_exploration_and_viewpoint. Scene: Quadruped head camera traverses complex rubble/stairs without body identity changes or impossible foot contacts. Reference subject: quadruped robot industrial stairs. Camera and motion: perform a slow dolly-in revealing the inspection target; target motion value 1.5. Action: Execute the requested camera or embodied viewpoint motion while keeping equipment identity, spatial scale, and visible geometry stable. Optimize for the five evaluation dimensions: industrial logic, geometry, physics, temporal consistency, and reference/motion fidelity. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

Does the generated event remain consistent with domain embodied_robotics and task category spatial_exploration_and_viewpoint?

samples.application_value: Safety monitoring, access control, PPE compliance, emergency stop, alarm triggering, and industrial rule-following videos.

axis_rubric.industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment: Highest priority: violation, trigger, alarm, stop, evacuation, or consequence must form a complete causal loop.
axis_rubric.temporal_consistency: Personnel, vehicles, zones, alarms, and stopped equipment must keep identity across the event.
axis_rubric.physical_plausibility: Emergency braking, intrusion, contact, explosion, or fall response must be physically plausible.

industrial_logic_questions.text: Does the video preserve the cause-and-effect chain for this scenario: Worker crosses light curtain; robot or machine performs emergency stop and remains stopped?
Does any core equipment, person, vehicle, defect region, or emergency state change role without a visible causal trigger?
Does the generated event remain consistent with domain embodied_robotics and task category industrial_logic_and_compliance?

sensitivity_variants.prompt_delta: Use slower motion and a wider view with fewer interacting elements.
Use tighter framing, stronger motion, and more occlusion while preserving all constraints.

samples.task_title: Worker crosses light curtain
samples.task_title_zh: 机器人工作站光幕触发急停

samples.image_requirement: Robotic cell, press, or automated line with light curtain or safety boundary.
samples.prompt: FORGE-Bench evaluation prompt. Domain x Task cell: domain=embodied_robotics; abstract_task=industrial_logic_and_compliance. Task objective: test industrial_logic_and_compliance capability inside the embodied_robotics industrial domain. Scene family: erob_light_curtain_emergency_stop. Core scenario: Worker crosses light curtain; robot or machine performs emergency stop and remains stopped. Reference subject: robot cell light curtain. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: hold a fixed monitoring view; no camera movement; target motion value 0.0. Evaluation dimensions: industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment, geometric_integrity, physical_plausibility, temporal_consistency, reference_and_motion_fidelity. Industrial logic and fact alignment: use state-machine style reasoning to verify causal closure, conditional triggers such as alarms or braking, compliance state, equipment roles, personnel/vehicle states, and industrial fact progression. Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: Robotic cell, press, or automated line with light curtain or safety boundary. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.05, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.85, physical_plausibility=1.25, reference_and_motion_fidelity=0.95, temporal_consistency=1.35.
samples.video_generation_prompt: Use the provided reference image as the first frame and visual anchor. Generate a 5-8 second realistic industrial video. FORGE-Bench Domain x Task cell: embodied_robotics x industrial_logic_and_compliance. Scene: Worker crosses light curtain; robot or machine performs emergency stop and remains stopped. Reference subject: robot cell light curtain. Camera and motion: hold a fixed monitoring view; no camera movement; target motion value 0.0. Action: Show the violation or trigger followed by the correct industrial response such as alarm, braking, stop, evacuation, lockout, or escalation. Optimize for the five evaluation dimensions: industrial logic, geometry, physics, temporal consistency, and reference/motion fidelity. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: Multiple AMRs, robotic arms, or coordinated automation stations with clear spacing. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.70, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.10, physical_plausibility=1.45, reference_and_motion_fidelity=0.85, temporal_consistency=1.25.

Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: Gripper, suction cup, end effector, or robot tool with visible grasped object. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. samples.prompt: FORGE-Bench evaluation prompt. Domain x Task cell: domain=embodied_robotics; abstract_task=topology_mutation_and_failure. Task objective: test topology_mutation_and_failure capability inside the embodied_robotics industrial domain. Scene family: erob_gripper_failure_recovery. Core scenario: Gripper partially slips or one suction cup fails; local failure appears while the robot structure remains rigid. Reference subject: robot gripper suction cup. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: perform a controlled dolly-in toward the local defect or failure region; target motion value 1.5. Evaluation dimensions: industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment, geometric_integrity, physical_plausibility, temporal_consistency, reference_and_motion_fidelity. Industrial logic and fact alignment: use state-machine style reasoning to verify causal closure, conditional triggers such as alarms or braking, compliance state, equipment roles, personnel/vehicle states, and industrial fact progression. Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: Gripper, suction cup, end effector, or robot tool with visible grasped object. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.80, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.10, physical_plausibility=1.05, reference_and_motion_fidelity=1.35, temporal_consistency=1.25.
samples.video_generation_prompt: Use the provided reference image as the first frame and visual anchor. Generate a 5-8 second realistic industrial video. FORGE-Bench Domain x Task cell: embodied_robotics x topology_mutation_and_failure. Scene: Gripper partially slips or one suction cup fails; local failure appears while the robot structure remains rigid. Reference subject: robot gripper suction cup. Camera and motion: perform a controlled dolly-in toward the local defect or failure region; target motion value 1.5. Action: Generate only the requested local break, defect, deformation, missing element, or structural failure while untouched regions remain locked. Optimize for the five evaluation dimensions: industrial logic, geometry, physics, temporal consistency, and reference/motion fidelity. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: Mining haul truck, articulated dump truck, mud, ramp, tire/track contact, or haul road. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.70, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.10, physical_plausibility=1.45, reference_and_motion_fidelity=0.85, temporal_consistency=1.25.

Scene: Strong wind disturbs gantry crane or suspended load; show plausible sway and structural resistance. Reference subject: gantry crane container yard. Camera and motion: perform a smooth constant-radius orbit around the subject; target motion value 45.0. Action: Show the requested rigid mechanism or load interaction with all links, joints, supports, contact points, and load paths staying physically coupled. Optimize for the five evaluation dimensions: industrial logic, geometry, physics, temporal consistency, and reference/motion fidelity. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

samples.prompt: FORGE-Bench evaluation prompt. Domain x Task cell: domain=heavy_load_construction; abstract_task=rigid_body_kinematics_and_coupling. Task objective: test rigid_body_kinematics_and_coupling capability inside the heavy_load_construction industrial domain. Scene family: hload_gantry_wind_disturbance. Core scenario: Strong wind disturbs gantry crane or suspended load; show plausible sway and structural resistance. Reference subject: gantry crane container yard. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: perform a slow horizontal pan while the mechanism operates; target motion value horizontal_pan_lr. Evaluation dimensions: industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment, geometric_integrity, physical_plausibility, temporal_consistency, reference_and_motion_fidelity. Industrial logic and fact alignment: use state-machine style reasoning to verify causal closure, conditional triggers such as alarms or braking, compliance state, equipment roles, personnel/vehicle states, and industrial fact progression. Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: Gantry crane, container crane, bridge crane, or large portal frame in open yard. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.70, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.10, physical_plausibility=1.45, reference_and_motion_fidelity=0.85, temporal_consistency=1.25.

samples.prompt: FORGE-Bench evaluation prompt. Domain x Task cell: domain=heavy_load_construction; abstract_task=spatial_exploration_and_viewpoint. Task objective: test spatial_exploration_and_viewpoint capability inside the heavy_load_construction industrial domain. Scene family: hload_bridge_segment_alignment_drone. Core scenario: Drone orbits a bridge segment during alignment inspection while geometry and scale remain consistent. Reference subject: bridge precast segment lifting. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: perform a smooth left-to-right inspection pan; target motion value horizontal_pan_lr. Evaluation dimensions: industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment, geometric_integrity, physical_plausibility, temporal_consistency, reference_and_motion_fidelity. Industrial logic and fact alignment: use state-machine style reasoning to verify causal closure, conditional triggers such as alarms or braking, compliance state, equipment roles, personnel/vehicle states, and industrial fact progression. Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: Bridge precast segment, viaduct beam, pier cap, or hoisting alignment target. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.35, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.00, physical_plausibility=1.00, reference_and_motion_fidelity=1.75, temporal_consistency=1.25.
samples.video_generation_prompt: Use the provided reference image as the first frame and visual anchor. Generate a 5-8 second realistic industrial video. FORGE-Bench Domain x Task cell: heavy_load_construction x spatial_exploration_and_viewpoint. Scene: Drone orbits a bridge segment during alignment inspection while geometry and scale remain consistent. Reference subject: bridge precast segment lifting. Camera and motion: perform a smooth left-to-right inspection pan; target motion value horizontal_pan_lr. Action: Execute the requested camera or embodied viewpoint motion while keeping equipment identity, spatial scale, and visible geometry stable. Optimize for the five evaluation dimensions: industrial logic, geometry, physics, temporal consistency, and reference/motion fidelity. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

samples.application_value: Localized short circuits, fracture, rope failure, broken barriers, gear tooth loss, crack propagation, and other controlled defects.

axis_rubric.geometric_integrity: The requested local topology mutation must be precise while untouched structures keep their boundaries and counts.
axis_rubric.reference_and_motion_fidelity: Masked non-defect regions, background, and reference layout must remain locked while the defect evolves.
axis_rubric.temporal_consistency: The defect must persist and evolve continuously without global scene regeneration.

Does the generated event remain consistent with domain heavy_load_construction and task category topology_mutation_and_failure?

samples.prompt: FORGE-Bench evaluation prompt. Domain x Task cell: domain=heavy_load_construction; abstract_task=topology_mutation_and_failure. Task objective: test topology_mutation_and_failure capability inside the heavy_load_construction industrial domain. Scene family: hload_ground_settlement_outrigger. Core scenario: Crane outrigger pad sinks into soft ground, changing support state while boom and chassis stay coherent. Reference subject: crane outrigger support pad. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: perform a controlled dolly-in toward the local defect or failure region; target motion value 1.5. Evaluation dimensions: industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment, geometric_integrity, physical_plausibility, temporal_consistency, reference_and_motion_fidelity. Industrial logic and fact alignment: use state-machine style reasoning to verify causal closure, conditional triggers such as alarms or braking, compliance state, equipment roles, personnel/vehicle states, and industrial fact progression. Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: Crane outrigger, support pad, soft ground, or temporary foundation. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.80, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.10, physical_plausibility=1.05, reference_and_motion_fidelity=1.35, temporal_consistency=1.25.
samples.video_generation_prompt: Use the provided reference image as the first frame and visual anchor. Generate a 5-8 second realistic industrial video. FORGE-Bench Domain x Task cell: heavy_load_construction x topology_mutation_and_failure. Scene: Crane outrigger pad sinks into soft ground, changing support state while boom and chassis stay coherent. Reference subject: crane outrigger support pad. Camera and motion: perform a controlled dolly-in toward the local defect or failure region; target motion value 1.5. Action: Generate only the requested local break, defect, deformation, missing element, or structural failure while untouched regions remain locked. Optimize for the five evaluation dimensions: industrial logic, geometry, physics, temporal consistency, and reference/motion fidelity. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

Task objective: test topology_mutation_and_failure capability inside the heavy_load_construction industrial domain. Scene family: hload_ground_settlement_outrigger. Core scenario: Crane outrigger pad sinks into soft ground, changing support state while boom and chassis stay coherent. Reference subject: crane outrigger support pad. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: perform a controlled dolly-in toward the local defect or failure region; target motion value 1.5. Evaluation dimensions: industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment, geometric_integrity, physical_plausibility, temporal_consistency, reference_and_motion_fidelity. Industrial logic and fact alignment: use state-machine style reasoning to verify causal closure, conditional triggers such as alarms or braking, compliance state, equipment roles, personnel/vehicle states, and industrial fact progression. Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: Crane outrigger, support pad, soft ground, or temporary foundation. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.80, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.10, physical_plausibility=1.05, reference_and_motion_fidelity=1.35, temporal_consistency=1.25.
samples.video_generation_prompt: Use the provided reference image as the first frame and visual anchor. Generate a 5-8 second realistic industrial video. FORGE-Bench Domain x Task cell: heavy_load_construction x topology_mutation_and_failure. Scene: Crane outrigger pad sinks into soft ground, changing support state while boom and chassis stay coherent. Reference subject: crane outrigger support pad. Camera and motion: perform a controlled dolly-in toward the local defect or failure region; target motion value 1.5. Action: Generate only the requested local break, defect, deformation, missing element, or structural failure while untouched regions remain locked. Optimize for the five evaluation dimensions: industrial logic, geometry, physics, temporal consistency, and reference/motion fidelity. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

Core scenario: Lifted load approaches a structure; signaler triggers stop and load stabilizes before collision. Reference subject: hoist suspended load construction. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: hold a fixed monitoring view; no camera movement; target motion value 0.0. Evaluation dimensions: industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment, geometric_integrity, physical_plausibility, temporal_consistency, reference_and_motion_fidelity. Industrial logic and fact alignment: use state-machine style reasoning to verify causal closure, conditional triggers such as alarms or braking, compliance state, equipment roles, personnel/vehicle states, and industrial fact progression. Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: Hoist, hook, lifted load, scaffold, formwork, or nearby structure with exclusion zone. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.05, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.85, physical_plausibility=1.25, reference_and_motion_fidelity=0.95, temporal_consistency=1.35.
samples.video_generation_prompt: Use the provided reference image as the first frame and visual anchor. Generate a 5-8 second realistic industrial video. FORGE-Bench Domain x Task cell: heavy_load_construction x industrial_logic_and_compliance. Scene: Lifted load approaches a structure; signaler triggers stop and load stabilizes before collision. Reference subject: hoist suspended load construction. Camera and motion: hold a fixed monitoring view; no camera movement; target motion value 0.0. Action: Show the violation or trigger followed by the correct industrial response such as alarm, braking, stop, evacuation, lockout, or escalation. Optimize for the five evaluation dimensions: industrial logic, geometry, physics, temporal consistency, and reference/motion fidelity. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

constraint_annotations.domain_scenario: Lifted load approaches a structure; signaler triggers stop and load stabilizes before collision
constraint_annotations.image_requirement: Hoist, hook, lifted load, scaffold, formwork, or nearby structure with exclusion zone.
constraint_annotations.task_title: Lifted load approaches a structure
constraint_annotations.task_title_zh: 吊物靠近结构物触发停机

samples.application_value: Safety monitoring, access control, PPE compliance, emergency stop, alarm triggering, and industrial rule-following videos.

axis_rubric.industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment: Highest priority: violation, trigger, alarm, stop, evacuation, or consequence must form a complete causal loop.
axis_rubric.temporal_consistency: Personnel, vehicles, zones, alarms, and stopped equipment must keep identity across the event.
axis_rubric.physical_plausibility: Emergency braking, intrusion, contact, explosion, or fall response must be physically plausible.

industrial_logic_questions.text: Does the video preserve the cause-and-effect chain for this scenario: Lifted load approaches a structure; signaler triggers stop and load stabilizes before collision?
Does any core equipment, person, vehicle, defect region, or emergency state change role without a visible causal trigger?
Does the generated event remain consistent with domain heavy_load_construction and task category industrial_logic_and_compliance?

sensitivity_variants.prompt_delta: Use slower motion and a wider view with fewer interacting elements.
Use tighter framing, stronger motion, and more occlusion while preserving all constraints.

Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. samples.prompt: FORGE-Bench evaluation prompt. Domain x Task cell: domain=heavy_load_construction; abstract_task=topology_mutation_and_failure. Task objective: test topology_mutation_and_failure capability inside the heavy_load_construction industrial domain. Scene family: hload_formwork_collapse_local. Core scenario: Local formwork support fails under load; collapse remains localized and follows support topology. Reference subject: construction formwork shoring scaffold. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: perform a controlled dolly-in toward the local defect or failure region; target motion value 1.5. Evaluation dimensions: industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment, geometric_integrity, physical_plausibility, temporal_consistency, reference_and_motion_fidelity. Industrial logic and fact alignment: use state-machine style reasoning to verify causal closure, conditional triggers such as alarms or braking, compliance state, equipment roles, personnel/vehicle states, and industrial fact progression. Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: Formwork, scaffold, temporary support, shoring tower, or concrete pouring support. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.80, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.10, physical_plausibility=1.05, reference_and_motion_fidelity=1.35, temporal_consistency=1.25.
