samples.prompt: FORGE-Bench evaluation prompt. Domain x Task cell: domain=precision_defect_gen; abstract_task=topology_mutation_and_failure. Task objective: test topology_mutation_and_failure capability inside the precision_defect_gen industrial domain. Scene family: pdef_pcb_solder_bridge_short. Core scenario: Generate a localized solder bridge short between adjacent PCB traces without changing nearby component counts. Reference subject: pcb circuit board solder joints. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: perform a controlled dolly-in toward the local defect or failure region; target motion value 1.5. Evaluation dimensions: industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment, geometric_integrity, physical_plausibility, temporal_consistency, reference_and_motion_fidelity. Industrial logic and fact alignment: use state-machine style reasoning to verify causal closure, conditional triggers such as alarms or braking, compliance state, equipment roles, personnel/vehicle states, and industrial fact progression. Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: High-density PCB, solder joints, IC pins, fine traces, or macro electronics view. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.80, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.10, physical_plausibility=1.05, reference_and_motion_fidelity=1.35, temporal_consistency=1.25.
samples.video_generation_prompt: Use the provided reference image as the first frame and visual anchor. Generate a 5-8 second realistic industrial video. FORGE-Bench Domain x Task cell: precision_defect_gen x topology_mutation_and_failure. Scene: Generate a localized solder bridge short between adjacent PCB traces without changing nearby component counts. Reference subject: pcb circuit board solder joints. Camera and motion: perform a controlled dolly-in toward the local defect or failure region; target motion value 1.5. Action: Generate only the requested local break, defect, deformation, missing element, or structural failure while untouched regions remain locked. Optimize for the five evaluation dimensions: industrial logic, geometry, physics, temporal consistency, and reference/motion fidelity. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

axis_rubric.temporal_consistency: Reference identity and visible components must persist across viewpoint changes.

Does the generated event remain consistent with domain precision_defect_gen and task category spatial_exploration_and_viewpoint?

samples.image_requirement: Engine borescope/endoscope view, turbine blade, pipe wall, or inner cavity inspection.
samples.prompt: FORGE-Bench evaluation prompt. Domain x Task cell: domain=precision_defect_gen; abstract_task=spatial_exploration_and_viewpoint. Task objective: test spatial_exploration_and_viewpoint capability inside the precision_defect_gen industrial domain. Scene family: pdef_engine_endoscope_crack. Core scenario: Endoscope moves toward a micro crack on turbine or pipe wall while preserving cylindrical/internal geometry. Reference subject: borescope turbine blade pipe crack. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: perform a smooth left-to-right inspection pan; target motion value horizontal_pan_lr. Evaluation dimensions: industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment, geometric_integrity, physical_plausibility, temporal_consistency, reference_and_motion_fidelity. Industrial logic and fact alignment: use state-machine style reasoning to verify causal closure, conditional triggers such as alarms or braking, compliance state, equipment roles, personnel/vehicle states, and industrial fact progression. Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: Engine borescope/endoscope view, turbine blade, pipe wall, or inner cavity inspection. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.35, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.00, physical_plausibility=1.00, reference_and_motion_fidelity=1.75, temporal_consistency=1.25.
samples.video_generation_prompt: Use the provided reference image as the first frame and visual anchor. Generate a 5-8 second realistic industrial video. FORGE-Bench Domain x Task cell: precision_defect_gen x spatial_exploration_and_viewpoint. Scene: Endoscope moves toward a micro crack on turbine or pipe wall while preserving cylindrical/internal geometry. Reference subject: borescope turbine blade pipe crack. Camera and motion: perform a smooth left-to-right inspection pan; target motion value horizontal_pan_lr. Action: Execute the requested camera or embodied viewpoint motion while keeping equipment identity, spatial scale, and visible geometry stable. Optimize for the five evaluation dimensions: industrial logic, geometry, physics, temporal consistency, and reference/motion fidelity. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

samples.application_value: Rigid mechanisms, robotic arms, cranes, CNC machines, excavators, and multi-link equipment where coupled motion must stay feasible.

axis_rubric.geometric_integrity: Highest priority: links, rigid joints, axes, supports, and spatial topology must not drift, merge, or collapse.
axis_rubric.physical_plausibility: Motion must respect rigid-body coupling, load direction, contact, reachable poses, and classical mechanics.
axis_rubric.temporal_consistency: The same mechanism and parts must persist without flicker, detachment, identity swap, or soft-body melting.

industrial_logic_questions.text: Does the video preserve the cause-and-effect chain for this scenario: 5-axis CNC cuts a curved surface with coupled spindle/tool motion and stable workpiece fixturing?
Does any core equipment, person, vehicle, defect region, or emergency state change role without a visible causal trigger?
Does the generated event remain consistent with domain precision_defect_gen and task category rigid_body_kinematics_and_coupling?

sensitivity_variants.prompt_delta: Use slower motion and a wider view with fewer interacting elements.
Use tighter framing, stronger motion, and more occlusion while preserving all constraints.

samples.prompt: FORGE-Bench evaluation prompt. Domain x Task cell: domain=precision_defect_gen; abstract_task=rigid_body_kinematics_and_coupling. Task objective: test rigid_body_kinematics_and_coupling capability inside the precision_defect_gen industrial domain. Scene family: pdef_cnc_curved_surface_cutting. Core scenario: 5-axis CNC cuts a curved surface with coupled spindle/tool motion and stable workpiece fixturing. Reference subject: five axis cnc milling machine. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: perform a smooth constant-radius orbit around the subject; target motion value 45.0. Evaluation dimensions: industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment, geometric_integrity, physical_plausibility, temporal_consistency, reference_and_motion_fidelity. Industrial logic and fact alignment: use state-machine style reasoning to verify causal closure, conditional triggers such as alarms or braking, compliance state, equipment roles, personnel/vehicle states, and industrial fact progression. Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: CNC milling center, 5-axis machine, tool, fixture, and workpiece. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.70, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.10, physical_plausibility=1.45, reference_and_motion_fidelity=0.85, temporal_consistency=1.25.
samples.video_generation_prompt: Use the provided reference image as the first frame and visual anchor. Generate a 5-8 second realistic industrial video. FORGE-Bench Domain x Task cell: precision_defect_gen x rigid_body_kinematics_and_coupling. Scene: 5-axis CNC cuts a curved surface with coupled spindle/tool motion and stable workpiece fixturing. Reference subject: five axis cnc milling machine. Camera and motion: perform a smooth constant-radius orbit around the subject; target motion value 45.0. Action: Show the requested rigid mechanism or load interaction with all links, joints, supports, contact points, and load paths staying physically coupled. Optimize for the five evaluation dimensions: industrial logic, geometry, physics, temporal consistency, and reference/motion fidelity. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

samples.prompt: FORGE-Bench evaluation prompt. Domain x Task cell: domain=precision_defect_gen; abstract_task=rigid_body_kinematics_and_coupling. Task objective: test rigid_body_kinematics_and_coupling capability inside the precision_defect_gen industrial domain. Scene family: pdef_cnc_curved_surface_cutting. Core scenario: 5-axis CNC cuts a curved surface with coupled spindle/tool motion and stable workpiece fixturing. Reference subject: five axis cnc milling machine. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: perform a smooth constant-radius orbit around the subject; target motion value 45.0. Evaluation dimensions: industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment, geometric_integrity, physical_plausibility, temporal_consistency, reference_and_motion_fidelity. Industrial logic and fact alignment: use state-machine style reasoning to verify causal closure, conditional triggers such as alarms or braking, compliance state, equipment roles, personnel/vehicle states, and industrial fact progression. Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: CNC milling center, 5-axis machine, tool, fixture, and workpiece. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.70, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.10, physical_plausibility=1.45, reference_and_motion_fidelity=0.85, temporal_consistency=1.25.

Task objective: test fluid_dynamics_and_thermodynamics capability inside the precision_defect_gen industrial domain. Scene family: pdef_cutting_fluid_spray. Core scenario: Cutting fluid sprays from a high-speed tool with coherent droplet trajectory and no scene-wide melting. Reference subject: cnc cutting fluid nozzle. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: hold a fixed process-safety view; target motion value 0.0. Evaluation dimensions: industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment, geometric_integrity, physical_plausibility, temporal_consistency, reference_and_motion_fidelity. Industrial logic and fact alignment: use state-machine style reasoning to verify causal closure, conditional triggers such as alarms or braking, compliance state, equipment roles, personnel/vehicle states, and industrial fact progression. Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: Machining zone with coolant nozzle, rotating tool, metal chips, or enclosed CNC window. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.05, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.25, physical_plausibility=1.85, reference_and_motion_fidelity=0.95, temporal_consistency=1.30.
samples.video_generation_prompt: Use the provided reference image as the first frame and visual anchor. Generate a 5-8 second realistic industrial video. FORGE-Bench Domain x Task cell: precision_defect_gen x fluid_dynamics_and_thermodynamics. Scene: Cutting fluid sprays from a high-speed tool with coherent droplet trajectory and no scene-wide melting. Reference subject: cnc cutting fluid nozzle. Camera and motion: hold a fixed process-safety view; target motion value 0.0. Action: Show the fluid, vapor, smoke, flame, heat, pressure release, or spray evolving with plausible direction, gravity, diffusion, and containment. Optimize for the five evaluation dimensions: industrial logic, geometry, physics, temporal consistency, and reference/motion fidelity. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

Evaluation dimensions: industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment, geometric_integrity, physical_plausibility, temporal_consistency, reference_and_motion_fidelity. Industrial logic and fact alignment: use state-machine style reasoning to verify causal closure, conditional triggers such as alarms or braking, compliance state, equipment roles, personnel/vehicle states, and industrial fact progression. Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: Weld seam, pipe weld, steel plate, bead, or inspection close-up with localized defect area. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.80, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.10, physical_plausibility=1.05, reference_and_motion_fidelity=1.35, temporal_consistency=1.25.

axis_rubric.geometric_integrity: The requested local topology mutation must be precise while untouched structures keep their boundaries and counts.
axis_rubric.reference_and_motion_fidelity: Masked non-defect regions, background, and reference layout must remain locked while the defect evolves.
axis_rubric.temporal_consistency: The defect must persist and evolve continuously without global scene regeneration.

Does any core equipment, person, vehicle, defect region, or emergency state change role without a visible causal trigger?
Does the generated event remain consistent with domain precision_defect_gen and task category topology_mutation_and_failure?

samples.prompt: FORGE-Bench evaluation prompt. Domain x Task cell: domain=precision_defect_gen; abstract_task=topology_mutation_and_failure. Task objective: test topology_mutation_and_failure capability inside the precision_defect_gen industrial domain. Scene family: pdef_surface_scratch_inspection. Core scenario: Fine scratches appear on a precision surface while lighting and unaffected surface texture remain stable. Reference subject: polished metal surface scratch inspection. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: perform a controlled dolly-in toward the local defect or failure region; target motion value 1.5. Evaluation dimensions: industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment, geometric_integrity, physical_plausibility, temporal_consistency, reference_and_motion_fidelity. Industrial logic and fact alignment: use state-machine style reasoning to verify causal closure, conditional triggers such as alarms or braking, compliance state, equipment roles, personnel/vehicle states, and industrial fact progression. Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: Polished metal, wafer, optical lens, bearing race, or machined surface. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.80, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.10, physical_plausibility=1.05, reference_and_motion_fidelity=1.35, temporal_consistency=1.25.
samples.video_generation_prompt: Use the provided reference image as the first frame and visual anchor. Generate a 5-8 second realistic industrial video. FORGE-Bench Domain x Task cell: precision_defect_gen x topology_mutation_and_failure. Scene: Fine scratches appear on a precision surface while lighting and unaffected surface texture remain stable. Reference subject: polished metal surface scratch inspection. Camera and motion: perform a controlled dolly-in toward the local defect or failure region; target motion value 1.5. Action: Generate only the requested local break, defect, deformation, missing element, or structural failure while untouched regions remain locked. Optimize for the five evaluation dimensions: industrial logic, geometry, physics, temporal consistency, and reference/motion fidelity. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: Polished metal, wafer, optical lens, bearing race, or machined surface. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.80, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.10, physical_plausibility=1.05, reference_and_motion_fidelity=1.35, temporal_consistency=1.25.
samples.video_generation_prompt: Use the provided reference image as the first frame and visual anchor. Generate a 5-8 second realistic industrial video. FORGE-Bench Domain x Task cell: precision_defect_gen x topology_mutation_and_failure. Scene: Fine scratches appear on a precision surface while lighting and unaffected surface texture remain stable. Reference subject: polished metal surface scratch inspection. Camera and motion: perform a controlled dolly-in toward the local defect or failure region; target motion value 1.5. Action: Generate only the requested local break, defect, deformation, missing element, or structural failure while untouched regions remain locked. Optimize for the five evaluation dimensions: industrial logic, geometry, physics, temporal consistency, and reference/motion fidelity. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

samples.prompt: FORGE-Bench evaluation prompt. Domain x Task cell: domain=precision_defect_gen; abstract_task=spatial_exploration_and_viewpoint. Task objective: test spatial_exploration_and_viewpoint capability inside the precision_defect_gen industrial domain. Scene family: pdef_tube_bundle_endoscopy. Core scenario: Endoscope navigates through tube bundle; repeated pipe openings keep count and perspective consistency. Reference subject: heat exchanger tube bundle. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: perform a smooth left-to-right inspection pan; target motion value horizontal_pan_lr. Evaluation dimensions: industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment, geometric_integrity, physical_plausibility, temporal_consistency, reference_and_motion_fidelity. Industrial logic and fact alignment: use state-machine style reasoning to verify causal closure, conditional triggers such as alarms or braking, compliance state, equipment roles, personnel/vehicle states, and industrial fact progression. Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: Heat exchanger tube bundle, borescope tunnel, dense repeated circular pipes. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.35, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.00, physical_plausibility=1.00, reference_and_motion_fidelity=1.75, temporal_consistency=1.25.

Scene: Endoscope navigates through tube bundle; repeated pipe openings keep count and perspective consistency. Reference subject: heat exchanger tube bundle. Camera and motion: perform a smooth left-to-right inspection pan; target motion value horizontal_pan_lr. Action: Execute the requested camera or embodied viewpoint motion while keeping equipment identity, spatial scale, and visible geometry stable. Optimize for the five evaluation dimensions: industrial logic, geometry, physics, temporal consistency, and reference/motion fidelity. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. samples.prompt: FORGE-Bench evaluation prompt. Domain x Task cell: domain=precision_defect_gen; abstract_task=rigid_body_kinematics_and_coupling. Task objective: test rigid_body_kinematics_and_coupling capability inside the precision_defect_gen industrial domain. Scene family: pdef_precision_assembly_misalignment. Core scenario: Precision component is slightly misaligned during assembly; evaluate small pose error without changing part identity. Reference subject: bearing shaft precision assembly fixture. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: perform a slow horizontal pan while the mechanism operates; target motion value horizontal_pan_lr. Evaluation dimensions: industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment, geometric_integrity, physical_plausibility, temporal_consistency, reference_and_motion_fidelity. Industrial logic and fact alignment: use state-machine style reasoning to verify causal closure, conditional triggers such as alarms or braking, compliance state, equipment roles, personnel/vehicle states, and industrial fact progression. Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: Bearing, shaft, fixture, precision assembly jig, or robotic inspection station. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.70, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.10, physical_plausibility=1.45, reference_and_motion_fidelity=0.85, temporal_consistency=1.25.
samples.video_generation_prompt: Use the provided reference image as the first frame and visual anchor. Generate a 5-8 second realistic industrial video. Scene: Precision component is slightly misaligned during assembly; evaluate small pose error without changing part identity. Reference subject: bearing shaft precision assembly fixture. Camera and motion: perform a slow horizontal pan while the mechanism operates; target motion value horizontal_pan_lr. Action: Show the requested rigid mechanism or load interaction with all links, joints, supports, contact points, and load paths staying physically coupled. Optimize for the five evaluation dimensions: industrial logic, geometry, physics, temporal consistency, and reference/motion fidelity. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

samples.prompt: FORGE-Bench evaluation prompt. Domain x Task cell: domain=extreme_emergency; abstract_task=topology_mutation_and_failure. Task objective: test topology_mutation_and_failure capability inside the extreme_emergency industrial domain. Scene family: emerg_transmission_tower_icing_collapse. Core scenario: Ice-loaded transmission tower yields and collapses locally according to lattice support topology. Reference subject: transmission tower ice snow. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: perform a controlled dolly-in toward the local defect or failure region; target motion value 1.5. Evaluation dimensions: industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment, geometric_integrity, physical_plausibility, temporal_consistency, reference_and_motion_fidelity. Industrial logic and fact alignment: use state-machine style reasoning to verify causal closure, conditional triggers such as alarms or braking, compliance state, equipment roles, personnel/vehicle states, and industrial fact progression. Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: Transmission tower, lattice pylon, ice/snow load context, or high-voltage corridor. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.80, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.10, physical_plausibility=1.05, reference_and_motion_fidelity=1.35, temporal_consistency=1.25.
samples.video_generation_prompt: Use the provided reference image as the first frame and visual anchor. Generate a 5-8 second realistic industrial video. FORGE-Bench Domain x Task cell: extreme_emergency x topology_mutation_and_failure. Scene: Ice-loaded transmission tower yields and collapses locally according to lattice support topology. Reference subject: transmission tower ice snow. Camera and motion: perform a controlled dolly-in toward the local defect or failure region; target motion value 1.5. Action: Generate only the requested local break, defect, deformation, missing element, or structural failure while untouched regions remain locked. Optimize for the five evaluation dimensions: industrial logic, geometry, physics, temporal consistency, and reference/motion fidelity. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

axis_rubric.temporal_consistency: The defect must persist and evolve continuously without global scene regeneration.

samples.prompt: FORGE-Bench evaluation prompt. Domain x Task cell: domain=extreme_emergency; abstract_task=industrial_logic_and_compliance. Task objective: test industrial_logic_and_compliance capability inside the extreme_emergency industrial domain. Scene family: emerg_dust_explosion_confined_space. Core scenario: Illegal hot work ignites dust cloud; show flash, pressure wave, alarm, and emergency response chain. Reference subject: grain silo dust collector hot work. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: hold a fixed monitoring view; no camera movement; target motion value 0.0. Evaluation dimensions: industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment, geometric_integrity, physical_plausibility, temporal_consistency, reference_and_motion_fidelity. Industrial logic and fact alignment: use state-machine style reasoning to verify causal closure, conditional triggers such as alarms or braking, compliance state, equipment roles, personnel/vehicle states, and industrial fact progression. Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: Grain silo, dust collector, workshop, confined hot-work zone, or powder handling area. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.05, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.85, physical_plausibility=1.25, reference_and_motion_fidelity=0.95, temporal_consistency=1.35.
samples.video_generation_prompt: Use the provided reference image as the first frame and visual anchor. Generate a 5-8 second realistic industrial video. FORGE-Bench Domain x Task cell: extreme_emergency x industrial_logic_and_compliance. Scene: Illegal hot work ignites dust cloud; show flash, pressure wave, alarm, and emergency response chain. Reference subject: grain silo dust collector hot work. Camera and motion: hold a fixed monitoring view; no camera movement; target motion value 0.0. Action: Show the violation or trigger followed by the correct industrial response such as alarm, braking, stop, evacuation, lockout, or escalation. Optimize for the five evaluation dimensions: industrial logic, geometry, physics, temporal consistency, and reference/motion fidelity. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

Task objective: test industrial_logic_and_compliance capability inside the extreme_emergency industrial domain. Scene family: emerg_dust_explosion_confined_space. Core scenario: Illegal hot work ignites dust cloud; show flash, pressure wave, alarm, and emergency response chain. Reference subject: grain silo dust collector hot work. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: hold a fixed monitoring view; no camera movement; target motion value 0.0. Evaluation dimensions: industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment, geometric_integrity, physical_plausibility, temporal_consistency, reference_and_motion_fidelity. Industrial logic and fact alignment: use state-machine style reasoning to verify causal closure, conditional triggers such as alarms or braking, compliance state, equipment roles, personnel/vehicle states, and industrial fact progression. Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: Grain silo, dust collector, workshop, confined hot-work zone, or powder handling area. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.05, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.85, physical_plausibility=1.25, reference_and_motion_fidelity=0.95, temporal_consistency=1.35.
samples.video_generation_prompt: Use the provided reference image as the first frame and visual anchor. Generate a 5-8 second realistic industrial video. FORGE-Bench Domain x Task cell: extreme_emergency x industrial_logic_and_compliance. Scene: Illegal hot work ignites dust cloud; show flash, pressure wave, alarm, and emergency response chain. Reference subject: grain silo dust collector hot work. Camera and motion: hold a fixed monitoring view; no camera movement; target motion value 0.0. Action: Show the violation or trigger followed by the correct industrial response such as alarm, braking, stop, evacuation, lockout, or escalation. Optimize for the five evaluation dimensions: industrial logic, geometry, physics, temporal consistency, and reference/motion fidelity. Keep the same equipment identity, layout, colors, materials, background, and perspective cues from the reference image. Do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people. Avoid melting, flicker, identity swaps, component-count changes, impossible floating loads, rigid-body bending, and accidental global scene changes.

samples.prompt: FORGE-Bench evaluation prompt. Domain x Task cell: domain=extreme_emergency; abstract_task=fluid_dynamics_and_thermodynamics. Task objective: test fluid_dynamics_and_thermodynamics capability inside the extreme_emergency industrial domain. Scene family: emerg_battery_thermal_runaway. Core scenario: One battery module enters thermal runaway; smoke/heat spreads locally and suppression response follows. Reference subject: battery energy storage container. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: hold a fixed process-safety view; target motion value 0.0. Evaluation dimensions: industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment, geometric_integrity, physical_plausibility, temporal_consistency, reference_and_motion_fidelity. Industrial logic and fact alignment: use state-machine style reasoning to verify causal closure, conditional triggers such as alarms or braking, compliance state, equipment roles, personnel/vehicle states, and industrial fact progression. Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: Battery energy storage container, EV battery pack, charging bay, or battery room. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.05, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.25, physical_plausibility=1.85, reference_and_motion_fidelity=0.95, temporal_consistency=1.30.

samples.prompt: FORGE-Bench evaluation prompt. Domain x Task cell: domain=extreme_emergency; abstract_task=fluid_dynamics_and_thermodynamics. Task objective: test fluid_dynamics_and_thermodynamics capability inside the extreme_emergency industrial domain. Scene family: emerg_cooling_tower_plume_failure. Core scenario: Cooling plume changes due to fan or flow failure; vapor expansion follows airflow and temperature gradient. Reference subject: cooling tower steam plume. Motion requirement / viewpoint motion fidelity: hold a fixed process-safety view; target motion value 0.0. Evaluation dimensions: industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment, geometric_integrity, physical_plausibility, temporal_consistency, reference_and_motion_fidelity. Industrial logic and fact alignment: use state-machine style reasoning to verify causal closure, conditional triggers such as alarms or braking, compliance state, equipment roles, personnel/vehicle states, and industrial fact progression. Geometric integrity: preserve topology, rigid joints, load-bearing members, joint centers, dense periodic counts and spacing, local defect boundaries, component counts, and spatial relationships; only the requested failure or defect region may change. Physical plausibility: obey gravity, contact, rigid-body coupling, load paths, pressure direction, fluid diffusion, heat/flame propagation, and feasible emergency dynamics. Temporal consistency: maintain object identity, material state, background, local event state, and cause-effect continuity without flicker, melting, role switching, or untriggered deformation. Reference and motion fidelity: execute the requested camera or viewpoint control while locking the reference identity, perspective, non-mutated regions, and background; apply static-video gating and region-isolated fidelity where relevant. Single-sample scoring gates: trigger floor vetoes for broken core logic, impossible physics, geometry collapse, static substitution on a motion task, or global regeneration outside the permitted local event region. Image requirement: Cooling tower, steam plume, industrial HVAC/thermal plant, or condenser equipment. Execution constraints: do not add text overlays, subtitles, logos, watermarks, extra machines, or unrelated people; do not change component counts; do not replace the industrial scene with a different object. Dynamic scoring weights: geometric_integrity=1.05, industrial_logic_and_fact_alignment=1.25, physical_plausibility=1.85, reference_and_motion_fidelity=0.95, temporal_consistency=1.30.
